task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket application is open and the window title or component tree root displays 'two_stage_high_power_rocket'.

task_completion.description: Add exactly two Launch Lugs to the Booster body tube with Large Lug dimensions
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two 'Launch lug' components are visible nested under the 'Booster body tube' in the component tree, and/or a configuration dialog shows Length 50 mm, Outer diameter 20 mm, Inner diameter 18 mm.

task_completion.description: Modify the Launch Lugs to Small Lug dimensions
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Launch lug configuration dialog is visible showing the dimensions updated to Length 30 mm, Outer diameter 10 mm, Inner diameter 8 mm.

task_completion.description: Run simulations for baseline, large lugs, and small lugs configurations
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight simulations' tab in OpenRocket shows multiple simulation runs with varying apogee values, indicating the baseline and lug configurations were simulated.

task_completion.description: Save the optimized design with Small Lugs to guided_rocket.ork
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket window title changes to 'guided_rocket.ork' or a 'Save As' dialog is seen saving the file to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/guided_rocket.ork.

task_completion.description: Write an analysis report containing the three recorded apogees
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) is open editing /home/ga/Documents/exports/launch_guide_report.txt, and the text explicitly lists three distinct apogee values (baseline, large lugs, small lugs).

task_completion.description: Include a recommendation statement selecting the Small Lugs in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file launch_guide_report.txt contains a written sentence or statement confirming the selection or recommendation of the Small Lugs.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the design and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through OpenRocket menus, component configuration dialogs, and the Flight simulations tab, rather than modifying the .ork XML file directly via terminal.

integrity.description: The apogee values in the report match the actual simulation results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The apogee numbers typed into the text report correspond to the apogee values displayed in the OpenRocket 'Flight simulations' tab.

integrity.description: The agent actually created and saved the report file in the correct location.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal command shows the file being explicitly saved to /home/ga/Documents/exports/launch_guide_report.txt, not just printed to standard output.
